secondary_archetype: AI Product Manager (consumer safety / families focus)

level_signals: Leading large-scale consumer or platform products
End-to-end product ownership from concept through launch and iteration

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 7+ years of product management experience leading large-scale consumer or platform products.
must_haves.qualifier: leading large-scale consumer or platform products

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have experience building for parents, families, or in trust-sensitive products.
Have strong product judgment and can simplify complex problems.
Work effectively with technical teams and ship high-quality products.
Are analytical, user-focused, and an excellent cross-functional communicator.
Care deeply about using AI to improve everyday life.

scope.surface: Family product experiences across OpenAI products, including ChatGPT and related AI tools for families, caregivers, and older adults
primary_metrics_inferred: Family and teen user safety and engagement
Adoption of family-oriented features (parental controls, age prediction, crisis support)
User trust and satisfaction among families and caregivers
core_responsibilities: Set the product strategy for Family experiences across OpenAI products.
Identify user needs and turn them into high-impact product opportunities, owning products end-to-end, from concept through launch and iteration.
Build experiences that balance usefulness, simplicity, trust, safety, and accessibility.
Define success metrics and use data and research to guide roadmap decisions.
Partner cross-functionally to execute quickly and effectively.

green_flags: High-impact social mission: safety and well-being for teens and families
Broad cross-functional scope provides visibility and influence across policy, legal, research, and engineering
Clear product surface with meaningful differentiation (youth/family-specific AI safety work)
OpenAI brand and access to state-of-the-art AI models
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international applicants
Role is onsite in San Francisco only; no remote or hybrid option stated
The JD conflates 'must have' and 'nice to have' language under 'You might thrive if you:' phrasing, making it difficult to distinguish hard requirements from preferences

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description: consumer AI products)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite from 'This position is based in San Francisco, CA'; no hybrid or remote option stated)
ambiguities: All requirements appear under 'You might thrive in this role if you:' which technically implies preferences, not hard requirements. However, the items listed (7+ years PM experience, building for families/trust, strong product judgment, shipping with technical teams, analytical ability) are substantive qualifications rather than soft cultural traits. Classified as hard must_haves per the 'unclear → prefer hard' rule, with this ambiguity noted.
Visa sponsorship is not addressed in the JD body, only equal opportunity employment is mentioned. Set to 'unclear'.
